question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls or influences the stake values reported for a subset of nodes cause partialCollection() to consistently exclude ≥30% of honest nodes from signature collection by ensuring high-stake nodes appear first in the shuffled list, so that neededStake is met before those nodes are included, causing the importer to process only a minority-stake subset and stall consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that partialCollection() reads totalStake from nodes.getFirst().getTotalStake() (a single node's reported value) rather than summing all node stakes, so that if the first node in the shuffled list reports an inflated totalStake, neededStake is set too high and the while-loop walks off the end of the nodes list causing an IndexOutOfBoundsException that crashes the download batch, effectively halting processing for ≥30% of files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user who can inject malformed signature files into the cloud storage bucket for ≥30% of nodes cause onErrorContinue() to silently swallow all parsing errors for those nodes, resulting in their signatures being absent from sigFilesMap, so that consensus cannot be reached and the importer stalls indefinitely without any alerting beyond a log error?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Flux.timeout() in downloadAndParseSigFiles() to fire by making cloud storage requests for ≥30% of nodes hang indefinitely (e.g., via slow-response injection at the storage layer), so that the entire signature collection batch times out and no signatures are collected, halting importer progress for every subsequent polling cycle?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user cause SignatureVerificationException to be thrown for every filename group in sigFilesMap by ensuring that signatures from ≥30% of nodes are absent or malformed, so that the final group throws the exception which propagates up to downloadNextBatch() and is only logged as a warning, causing the importer to silently skip the entire batch and never advance lastStreamFile?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user who can write to the cloud storage bucket for one node upload a stream file whose getPreviousHash() returns the all-zeros SHA-384 empty hash string, causing SHA_384.isHashEmpty() to return true and verifyHashChain() to return true unconditionally, thereby bypassing the hash chain integrity check and allowing a forged file to be accepted as valid, which corrupts the mirror node's view of the ledger state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition where lastStreamFile is empty (expectedPreviousHash is null) causing SHA_384.isHashEmpty(null) to return true and verifyHashChain() to skip chain validation entirely, so that on importer startup or after a restart any file with any previousHash is accepted without chain verification, allowing injection of a file that breaks the hash chain for all subsequent files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user cause verifySignatures() to return false for every CONSENSUS_REACHED signature by making streamFileProvider.get(dataFilename).block() throw a TransientProviderException for all nodes, so that the catch block logs a warning and continues to the next signature, exhausting all candidates and returning false, which causes the importer to log an error but not advance lastStreamFile, permanently stalling processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user who can write to the cloud storage bucket upload a data file that causes streamFileReader.read(streamFileData) to throw an unchecked Exception (not FileOperationException/HashMismatchException/TransientProviderException), which is caught by the generic catch block that only logs an error and tries the next node, so that if all CONSENSUS_REACHED nodes serve a malformed file the importer silently fails to process the batch and stalls?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: downloadNextBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a permanent self-disable of the downloader by causing downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) to be called inside verifySignatures() when dataFilename.getInstant().isAfter(endDate) evaluates to true, by uploading a signature file with a future timestamp to the cloud bucket, so that the importer permanently stops polling and processing all stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: downloadNextBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DEMO network check in downloadNextBatch() where an empty sigFilesMap causes downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) to be called, by manipulating the cloud storage bucket to appear empty (e.g., deleting or hiding all signature files), causing the importer to permanently disable itself and stop processing all stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: statusMap()] Can an unprivileged external user cause unbounded memory growth in nodeSignatureStatusMetricMap (a ConcurrentHashMap<Long, Counter>) by flooding the system with signature files from a large number of distinct fake node IDs, causing a new Counter to be created for each unique nodeId via computeIfAbsent(), eventually exhausting JVM heap memory and crashing the importer process?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: statusMap()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the MeterRegistry to be flooded with new Counter registrations by repeatedly triggering statusMap() with signatures from many distinct node IDs, causing the metrics system to exhaust memory or file descriptors, degrading or crashing the importer service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getStartAfterFilename()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getStartAfterFilename() falls back to StreamFilename.EPOCH when lastStreamFile is empty, causing the importer to re-download and re-process all historical stream files from the beginning on every restart, creating a sustained CPU and I/O denial-of-service that prevents the importer from catching up to the current chain tip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a NullPointerException or arithmetic overflow in onVerified() by uploading a stream file with a consensusStart value of Long.MIN_VALUE, causing the subtraction streamFile.getConsensusStart() - last.getConsensusStart() to overflow and produce a negative latency value that corrupts the streamCloseMetric timer, potentially crashing the metrics recording?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a NullPointerException in onVerified() by uploading a stream file where getConsensusEnd() returns 0 or a negative value, causing Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, streamFile.getConsensusEnd()) to produce an invalid Instant that makes Duration.between() throw an exception, crashing the batch and preventing lastStreamFile from being updated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a long overflow in setStreamFileIndex() by manipulating the lastStreamFile to have an index of Long.MAX_VALUE, so that the map(v -> v + 1) operation overflows to Long.MIN_VALUE, assigning a negative index to the new stream file and corrupting the block index sequence in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass the file hash verification in verify() by uploading a data file where getFileHash() returns null, causing Objects.equals(null, signature.getFileHashAsHex()) to return false and throw a HashMismatchException, but if the signature's fileHashAsHex is also null, Objects.equals(null, null) returns true and the check passes, allowing a file with no hash to be accepted as valid?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: verify()] Can an unprivileged external user cause verify() to throw a HashMismatchException for every node's data file by uploading a stream file whose getMetadataHash() does not match the signature's metadataHashAsHex, so that all CONSENSUS_REACHED signatures fail verification, verifySignatures() returns false, and the importer permanently stalls on that filename without advancing lastStreamFile?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a memory exhaustion attack by uploading an extremely large number of signature files to the cloud storage bucket, causing the Flux.collect() in downloadAndParseSigFiles() to accumulate all signatures into the in-memory TreeMultimap before any processing occurs, exhausting JVM heap and crashing the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user cause CPU exhaustion by uploading signature files with filenames that are lexicographically identical but differ in content, causing the TreeMultimap's STREAM_FILE_SIGNATURE_COMPARATOR to perform excessive Ordering.arbitrary() comparisons during insertion, degrading performance and causing the importer to fall behind the chain tip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user cause an infinite loop or hang in partialCollection() by manipulating node stake values so that every node reports a stake of 0 (when totalStake > 0), causing aggregateStake to never increase and the while loop condition aggregateStake < neededStake to never become false, hanging the download thread indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: partialCollection()] Can an unprivileged external user cause an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException in partialCollection() by manipulating the address book so that totalStake is reported as a very large value while all individual node stakes are 0, causing neededStake to be larger than the sum of all node stakes, so the while loop increments lastEntry past nodes.size() and nodes.get(lastEntry++) throws an exception?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ShutdownHelper.isStopping() is checked only at the top of the loop in verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles(), so that if a very large sigFilesMap is constructed (via many uploaded signature files), the loop runs for an extended period without checking the shutdown flag, preventing graceful shutdown and causing the JVM to forcibly terminate after the 10-second window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/Downloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: verifySigsAndDownloadDataFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the importer to skip processing of legitimate stream files by uploading a large number of fake signature files with filenames that sort lexicographically before the real files, causing the iterator to process all fake files first (each failing verification), exhausting the batch limit before reaching the real files?
